conceptualization.theme_description: Start your journey with vibrant underwater scenes.

techniques.name: Wet-on-Wet
techniques.description: Apply wet paint onto wet paper to create soft, flowing edges and allow colors to blend naturally, mimicking the fluid nature of water.

techniques.name: Salt Technique
techniques.description: Sprinkle salt on wet paint to create a textured effect that resembles small bubbles or the gritty texture of sand.

techniques.name: Dropping Technique
techniques.description: Drop concentrated spots of color into wet areas to represent marine life or plant forms.

techniques.name: Layering
techniques.description: Build up layers of transparent color to create depth and luminosity indicative of sunlight filtering through water.

techniques.name: Color Bleeding
techniques.description: Allow colors to bleed into each other to represent the dynamic and unpredictable forms of ocean currents and underwater landscapes.

elements.name: Coral Reefs
elements.description: Render blurred images of coral structures using combinations of pinks and oranges with touches of green.

elements.name: School of Fish
elements.description: Capture the essence of movement and shimmer, using silver and muted yellows, letting colors merge to suggest schools of fish.

elements.name: Seaweed
elements.description: Use vertical strokes of green and brown, allowing them to diffuse into the background, simulating undulating seaweed.

elements.name: Sunlight
elements.description: Represent sunlight using pale yellow and white, blurring the edges where light meets water to create a dappled effect.

conceptualization.visual_goals: The visual aim is to evoke the sensation of being submerged in a vivid, ethereal underwater landscape, full of color and seamless forms.
